title: The US imposes universal 50% tariffs on imports of semi-finished copper products and copper-intensive derivative products
bodyHtml:
President Donald J. Trump has signed a Proclamation, following Executive Order 14220 and a Section 232 investigation, to safeguard U.S. national security by addressing the impact of copper imports. The following measures will take effect on August 1, 2025:

- 50% universal tariffs will be imposed on imports of semi-finished copper products (e.g., pipes, wires, rods, sheets) and copper-intensive derivatives (e.g., fittings, cables, connectors, electrical components).

-Tariffs apply only to copper content; other materials are subject to standard duties. Tariffs do not stack with auto-related 232 tariffs.

- Raw copper materials and scrap (ores, cathodes, anodes, etc.) are exempt from these tariffs.

- Starting in 2027, a share of domestically produced copper materials (25%, then 30%, then 40%) must be sold within the U.S.

- 25% of high-quality copper scrap must also be sold domestically, with export licensing rules to follow.

Commerce will launch a process to include more copper-based products under the tariff scheme.

These measures aim to revitalize the domestic copper industry, ensure a secure supply chain, and reduce foreign dependency.

Executive Order:Adjusting Imports of Copper into the United States

Fact Sheet: President Donald J. Trump Takes Action to Address the Threat to National Security from Imports of Copper
